summary: This cluster investigates the photoprotective role of mycosporine-like amino acids (MAAs) in marine macroalgae, particularly in response to increasing solar UV-B radiation from stratospheric ozone depletion. Seven specific MAAs, including mycosporine-glycine, shinorine, porphyra-334, palythine, asterina-330, palythene, and usujirene, were identified with absorption spanning both UV-B and UV-A wavelengths. Research focused on Antarctic rhodophytes demonstrated that species with higher MAA concentrations exhibited greater tolerance to natural and artificial UV radiation, establishing a correlation between MAA content and UV sensitivity. Experimental evidence further indicated that MAA formation is regulated by both UV and photosynthetically active radiation (PAR), suggesting a complex physiological role beyond simple UV screening.
